scope_note: Teacher-facing prose swapped period -> unit. Schema field names (period_number, period_duration_minutes, period_numbers, periods[], period_ref) and scheduling/allocation vocabulary (period schedule, period budget, period count, per-period, period allocation) are RETAINED as 'period' by decision.
status: COMPLETE across the board (conservative). Awaiting: (1) constitution version bumps decision; (2) Lever B display-time cleaner (separate).

reversal.full: For each file, replay every 'swap' entry as after -> before, and delete each 'insert' entry's appended block. Restores byte-for-byte. `git checkout -- <path>` is the equivalent one-shot.
reversal.partial: Filter entries by file, by op, or by id and replay only that subset.

verification.method: git diff reviewed; grep confirmed no '-' line removed any period_number/period_duration/period_ref/period schedule/period budget/period count/per-period token.

not_changed_deliberately.assessment_constitutions: twau/prep, ss/middle, science/middle, science/secondary, mathematics/secondary — inspected; every 'period' is a schema key (period_number, period_ref, period_numbers) or scheduling ('stage period allocation', 'period budget', 'one item per period'). No teacher-facing lesson prose. Assessment items do not carry temporal cross-references, so no anchor added. Widen later if desired.
not_changed_deliberately.chapter_summary_prompts: science/secondary, english/{middle,prep,secondary}, maths/{middle,prep} — inspected; 'period' appears only as scheduling/authoring-meta ('allocate periods across sections', 'in-period task', 'beyond-period', 'period budgeting'). Not teacher-facing lesson prose. No change.
not_changed_deliberately.twau_competency_mapping: inspected; 'the periods that teach those sections' / 'period-level CG assignment' are scheduling/structural. No change.

vocab_anchor_text.standard: VOCABULARY (teacher-facing naming): The atomic teaching chunk is called a "unit" in ALL teacher-facing prose — teacher_notes, activity titles, homework, and any cross-reference to another chunk (e.g. "the previous unit", "this unit"). The token "period" is retained ONLY in (a) schema field names (period_number, period_duration_minutes, periods[], etc.) and (b) the scheduling/allocation budget (period schedule, period count, per-period budget). Never write "period" in prose the teacher reads.
vocab_anchor_text.twau_variant: same as standard but 'teacher_notes' replaced by 'teacher_facilitation_note'.

period's section and the chapter summary.

swaps.note: teacher_notes schema comment: 'recap what the previous period covered'
teacher_notes schema comment: 'Transition from prior period; preview into next'

files.note: teacher_notes guidance says 'transition from prior / preview next' with no 'period' token; anchor line governs the wording.
No teacher_notes cross-reference guidance; only 'this period's activity' in an activity_title schema comment (kept, schema). Anchor governs future prose.

teacher_facilitation_note has no chunk cross-reference; 'this period' appears only in schema field comments (implied_lo, section_context, textbook_anchor) — kept, structural. Anchor governs future prose.
